title: The Drug That Might Cure Addiction Has a Psychiatric Safety Problem Nobody Can Explain
description: Semaglutide is outperforming every approved addiction medication in large-scale data — cutting opioid overdose rates by 40% and AUD hospitalization by 36%. A separate cohort study found the same drug nearly doubles the risk of major depression. Both findings are real. Both are unexplained. This is what happens when a blockbuster drug outruns the science trying to understand it.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Drug That Might Cure Addiction Has a Psychiatric Safety Problem Nobody Can Explain
